message:
feat(transport): add experimental transport decision framework with fail-closed gating

Introduces a layered experimental transport system that allows preview
transport behaviors (multipath path selection, coded transport) to be
requested and logged without affecting the conservative production path
until explicitly gated in.

New types in transport/aggregator.rs:
- ExperimentalTransportGate: Disabled (default) | MultipathPreview
- TransportCodingPolicy: Disabled (default) | RaptorQFecPreview | RlncPreview
- ExperimentalTransportDowngradeReason: explains why preview was denied
  (gate disabled, RaptorQ closure pending)
- TransportExperimentContext: workload_id + benchmark_correlation_id for
  replayable decisions
- TransportExperimentDecision: full envelope with requested vs effective
  policies, path decision, downgrade reason, and log_fields() for
  structured artifact emission
- AggregatorConfig: bundles path_policy, experiment_gate, coding_policy

PathSelectionPolicy gains is_experimental_preview() to identify policies
that require the experimental gate.

TransportConfig in config.rs gains experiment_gate and coding_policy fields,
both defaulting to Disabled. New aggregator_config() method maps the public
config surface to internal AggregatorConfig with deterministic conservative
fallbacks. KV config parsing supports the new fields.

All experimental behavior is fail-closed: requesting preview features
without enabling the gate produces a TransportExperimentDecision that
records the downgrade reason and falls back to conservative behavior.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
